name: Mass continuity equation (local mass conservation)
principles: divergence theorem applied to a fixed control volume
conventions: rho is the local mass density (kg/m^3)
v is the local fluid velocity field (m/s)
Eulerian form: time derivative is at fixed spatial point, not following the parcel
no source / sink term assumed; with sources (e.g. evaporation): partial rho / partial t + div(rho v) = sigma
integral form over a control volume V: d/dt integral rho dV = -integral_{partial V} rho v . dA (mass leaving through surface = decrease inside)
valid in any continuum: gases, liquids, plasmas (with charge/momentum continuity as analogues)
expectedLimits: incompressible flow (rho = const): div v = 0; velocity field is solenoidal
steady state (partial rho / partial t = 0): div(rho v) = 0; mass flux through any closed surface vanishes
compressible flow with shocks: equation holds in weak (integral) form; strong form fails at the shock surface
1D flow in a duct of variable area A(x): rho v A = const along the duct (steady, single direction)
diffusive analogue (Fick's law): replace rho v with -D grad rho to get the diffusion equation
subsumes total mass conservation: integral of rho over all space is invariant in a closed system
references: Euler (1757) — Principes généraux du mouvement des fluides
Batchelor — An Introduction to Fluid Dynamics, ch.2
Anderson — Computational Fluid Dynamics: The Basics with Applications, ch.2
